zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros於1285年出生在英國越可爾爾姆斯利時，他的父親Sir William de Ros II31歲，母親Maud de Vaux25歲。他於1316年11月25日之前與Margery Badlesmere結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1343年2月3日在英國越可爾吉可哈姆享年58歲，埋葬在英國越可爾吉可哈姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros于1285年出生在英国越可爾爾姆斯利时，他的父亲Sir William de Ros II31岁，母亲Maud de Vaux25岁。他于1316年11月25日之前与Margery Badlesmere结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1343年2月3日在英国越可爾吉可哈姆享年58岁，埋葬在英国越可爾吉可哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros was born in 1285, in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir William de Ros II, was 31 and his mother, Maud de Vaux, was 25. He married Margery Badlesmere before 25 November 1316. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 3 February 1343, in Kirkham, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 58, and was buried in Kirkham, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros est né en 1285, à Helmsley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Ros II, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Maud de Vaux, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Margery Badlesmere avant 25 novembre 1316. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 3 février 1343, à Kirkham, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à Kirkham, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros 1285 in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William de Ros II 31 und seine Mutter Maud de Vaux 25. Er hat vor 25. November 1316 Margery Badlesmere geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 3. Februar 1343 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Kirkham, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Kirkham, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros è nato il nel 1285 nel Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Sir William de Ros II, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Maud de Vaux, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Margery Badlesmere prima del 25 novembre 1316. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 3 febbraio 1343, nel Kirkham, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolto a Kirkham, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Ros 2nd Baron de Rosが1285年にイギリスヨークシュアイアヘルムスリーで生まれた時，父Sir William de Ros IIは31歳，母Maud de Vauxは25歳であった。1316年11月25日以前にMargery Badlesmereと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1343年2月3日にイギリスヨークシュアイアキルクハムで58歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアキルクハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros(이)가 1285년 영국 요르크실르 헬슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William de Ros II(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud de Vaux(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1316년 11월 25일 이전 Margery Badlesmere(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1343년 2월 3일 영국 요르크실르 커카에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 커카에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros nasceu em 1285, em Helmsley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William de Ros II, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Vaux, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Margery Badlesmere antes 25 de novembro de 1316. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 3 de fevereiro de 1343, em Kirkham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 58 anos, e foi sepultado em Kirkham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros родился в 1285 г. в местности хелмсли, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William de Ros II было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Maud de Vaux, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Margery Badlesmere перед 25 ноябрь 1316 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 3 февраля 1343 г. в местности кирхам, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности кирхам, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros nació en 1285, en Helmsley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William de Ros II, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Maud de Vaux, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Margery Badlesmere antes de 25 de noviembre de 1316. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 3 de febrero de 1343, en Kirkham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultado en Kirkham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
